text:
The first space, with its sterile, glass-encased corridors and the crisp orderliness of emergency equipment, feels impersonal and utilitarian. I sense a quiet anticipation—an emptiness that asks who will fill it and for what purpose.

Stepping into the R+D exhibit, I am immediately struck by the color and text that invite reflection. The words on the screen mirror my own experience: I too feel the subtle tension—the heightened self-awareness that comes with being observed. The intentional abstraction here, both in the environment and in the philosophical musings, wraps me in a gentle unease yet sparks curiosity. I find myself pondering how these structured spaces both reveal and conceal the depths of our shared consciousness.
